name: claude-toolkit
description: BeSpunky's Claude Code skills, subagents and commands. Develop once here; install and continuously upgrade across all projects.

plugins.name: project-starter
plugins.description: Scaffold new BeSpunky-standard projects: integrated Nx monorepo + Angular (clean --minimal app, no demo) + devcontainer (Claude CLI & VS Code extension) + tailored CLAUDE.md. Generator-first.

plugins.name: engineering
plugins.description: BeSpunky engineering skills. Agnostic foundation (architect-mentality + architecture-first: solve through design, never a patch; fix the root cause; design + confirm refactors) plus technique domains with progressive-disclosure references: software-design, advanced-typescript, angular-architecture, angular-native-wrappers, nx-monorepo-and-dx.

plugins.name: browser-automation
plugins.description: Drive a real browser via Playwright (Chromium, pre-installed in BeSpunky devcontainers) — verify UI behavior end-to-end, reproduce bugs in the running app, capture before/after screenshots, scrape rendered DOM, generate test scaffolds via codegen. Loaded by default in scaffolded projects.

plugins.name: product-ux
plugins.description: BeSpunky product & UX design skills. keep-users-oriented: whenever you make someone wait or move them through a process, answer the three questions — what is the expected result, where am I, what is the next step — and pick the right feedback for deterministic (steps/progress) vs nondeterministic (estimate + notify) processes. A universal service-design principle, expressed primarily through software UI (loading/progress, async, multi-step flows, long-running jobs, notifications, optimistic UI). envision-the-experience: the upstream visioning move — imagine the world an interface lives in (its feeling, place, metaphor) before any layout or component, and design every element as a native inhabitant of that world; the screen is a place, not a page. Interrogate every element the spec names (a 'menu' might become a sunflower whose petals you pick), speak in sensory non-technical language that names no implementation, hold immersive back from busy (restraint over spectacle), and produce a Vision. envision-the-experience's matched building half is realize-the-vision: the craftsman that turns a Vision into a real, running interface by RESEARCHING the truest, soundest means before writing any code — reads the Vision as a contract (the feeling must survive), surveys the field (GSAP, Motion, three.js/R3F/angular-three, Web Animations, CSS scroll-driven animations, View Transitions, Lottie/Rive, Canvas/SVG/WebGL, Web Audio, haptics) and its caveats (jank, layout thrash, reduced-motion, accessibility, mobile), knows when to build in code vs. when to source an asset (figurative/photoreal art is generated, licensed, or downloaded — never hand-coded into path-soup — with licensing, review, and art-direction), chooses deliberately for the vision AND the constraints, designs and confirms the architecture before building (architecture-first), and verifies against the feeling in the running app — routing to a reference library of technique clusters.
